title: Vision loss - NHS
text:
like reading, using phones, and using the internet help with travelling and using transport advice and help with getting benefits help with adapting your workplace, or finding jobs support with learning at school, college or university talking therapies such as cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) or counselling Registering as sight impaired If you've been assessed by an ophthalmologist who says you have severe sight impairment (blind) or you're sight impaired (partially sighted), you can register as sight impaired with your local authority.
This can help you access low vision support and services in your area, and means you can apply for benefits.
Information: 
More information RNIB: registering as sight impaired RNIB: criteria for certification GOV.UK: blind person's allowance eligibility 
Royal National Institute of Blind People (RNIB) Royal National Institute of Blind People (RNIB) is a national charity that offers information and support for anyone affected by vision loss.
Website www.rnib.org.uk Helpline: 0303 123 9999 RNIB: practical and emotional support RNIB: coming to terms with sight loss
